summary: Default Config.BaseURL to HTTPS instead of HTTP so the CLI doesn't ship a cleartext-credential footgun.
reason: Greptile P1 (security) on PR #396: Load() set `BaseURL: "http://pad-dev.etherpad.org/api/1.3.1"` as the default, but `.printing-press.json` already registers the same host as `https://`, and pad-dev.etherpad.org serves the spec over TLS. Any user who runs the CLI without setting $ETHERPAD_BASE_URL would transmit their bearer token in cleartext to pad-dev.etherpad.org.
validated_outcome: Default is now https://. Local development instances that run on plain HTTP can still be reached by setting $ETHERPAD_BASE_URL explicitly. AuthURL/TokenURL helpers inherit the scheme from BaseURL, so the OIDC fallback path also defaults to HTTPS now.
upstream_issue: TODO: file on mvanhorn/cli-printing-press — generator template should default to HTTPS whenever the spec_url is HTTPS (and warn loudly if a printed CLI ships an HTTP default for a host that the spec serves over HTTPS).
